events.label: Pre-start engine problems eat the practice window
events.txt: Engine problems before the start that the crew had to fix; cost the practice window and Brad's bow assignment (he sailed as spinnaker trimmer instead). Time reconstructed — "before the starts," exact time unknown. Nature of the fault unrecorded.

events.label: Start — Class 6 ORC
events.txt: Derived exact from official results (finish minus elapsed = 12:35:00; whole fleet resolves to a clean ten-minute class ladder, 12:05 DH classes through 13:25 Class 12/13). No firsthand account of the start itself survives. Young American (reference boat, PHRF-DH) started 12:05 — thirty minutes earlier; handle the offset in any comparison.

events.label: Sailing without a working speedo (named post-race)
events.txt: The whole race was sailed without a functioning speedo (implied by Sebastian's Mon 13:16 message naming the fix: 'We will have better trim, speed and tactical decisions once we install a new Speedo and calibrate instruments'). Post-race insight, pinned at the start because it applied to every mile. Origin of the Bermuda instrument-calibration program.

events.label: South-of-rhumb positioning (the race's tactical bet)
events.txt: Positioned south of the rhumb line hoping for a south shift (Francois's slide: "a deliberate sacrifice"). Sailed 4.7 nm further than Christopher Dragon to reach the same layline; off-rhumb 5.12 + 1.34 = 6.5 nm vs CD's 1.70 (F's measurements, verify against track). Time reconstructed — anchored to F's "+8hrs" checkpoint (start + 8h); actual decision moment unrecorded. Decision-maker not on record; Brad was in no navigation conversations this race. Sebastian separately noted extra miles "on the way to Mountack and to Connecticut shore" (sic) — unquantified, undated, distinct from this excursion.

events.label: The post-race verdict on this stretch — the slides and the Honey rule
events.txt: Pinned where it applies: this south-of-rhumb stretch is what Francois's post-race slides quantified (circulated Mon May 25) — 'Because we are slower, extra distance hurts us more; we should per default stay close to rhumbline' — and what Sebastian's 13:06 reply turned into standing doctrine: 'only invest in going off rhumb line if you are sure you can recoup the investment plus bonus' (his Stan Honey paraphrase). Both are post-race analysis, re-timed to the water they describe.

events.label: 1BI rounding (reconstructed)
events.txt: Time reconstructed: start + Francois's "23 hours to 1BI" upwind figure — verify against track. Per F: arrived ~3 hours (~15 nm) after Christopher Dragon, ~1 hour after Young American. His split of the deficit at this mark: 2 hours boatspeed, 1 hour tactics.

events.label: The race's only spinnaker set
events.txt: Effectively the only set of the race, and it happened outside Brad's watch. Time reconstructed — presumed at/after the 1BI bear-away; actual time unrecorded. The kite was the A3 (confirmed up at the broach that night). One set + one douse total in 37.5 hours is itself a claim the track's maneuver signature can check.

events.label: Night build — fleet report (third-hand)
events.txt: A friend on a slower boat elsewhere in the fleet, relayed via the Max group chat: gusts to 35 kn after 2200, "a sea state on the Long Island Sound he has not experienced before," still overpowered under J4 and a second reef. Third-hand; not Ragana-measured wind. Onset differs from Ragana's own clock (next event) — two boats, two positions.

events.label: Night build — Ragana's clock
events.txt: Brad, contemporaneous: "Ragana had a rough time after the wind picked up at ~2300." A3 still flying. Approximate time, firsthand.

events.label: Douse discussed — and hesitated on
events.txt: The crew discussed dousing minutes before the broach and hesitated on the decision; the wind built further. (Brad, July 7 interview — firsthand.) Time reconstructed: "minutes before" the broach, which is itself bounded 2300–0205. This hesitation is the firsthand origin of the depower-early / change-by-trigger doctrine later codified in the crew primer and validated at Bermuda.

events.label: Broach (A3 up)
events.txt: Sequence finalized July 7: build → douse discussed → hesitation → further build → broach. A3 was up. Time reconstructed — window bounded by the ~2300 build and the 02:05 finish; wind seen aboard and helm at the moment not on record. The track's heading/SOG signature should pin this.

events.label: Emergency douse — all hands, kite saved
events.txt: Emergency douse immediately following the broach: all hands on deck, pre-assigned seats, Sebastian on the bow, Brad gathered the sail. A3 recovered with minor damage. Who voiced the post-douse seamanship praise is not on record (any public wording states the act only). Time reconstructed, coupled to the broach event.

events.label: Finish — 9th of 9 Class 6 finishers
events.txt: Official: 02:04:49 local. Elapsed 37:29:49; corrected 35:20:27 (ToT 0.9425). Last finisher in Class 6 ORC; 31st of 33 ORC-division finishers on the Block Island Course; +4:48:33 elapsed / +4:36:04 corrected behind class-and-division winner Christopher Dragon XII (same 12:35 start). Fourth-from-last of the course's 58 finishers across the line; 25 of 58 finished at or after 2300.

events.label: Delivery Stamford → Westport into the building breeze
events.txt: Departed soon after the finish because the wind was forecast to keep building; depart time reconstructed (~02:30–03:00). 10 nm took hours of slow beating against wind and current; dock lines on between 0700 and 0800. All six aboard including Brad — who was asleep the entire leg ("they let me sleep"). Not race data; ends the tracked story.
